info.title: BreachLens API
info.description:
Customer-facing scan API for BreachLens — trigger a scan, poll its status, and download SARIF. This is the same surface the `breachlens` CLI and the GitHub Action / GitLab templates drive.

**Base URL:** your own BreachLens deployment (self-hosted or hosted edge). Set the `baseUrl` server variable in the playground to your deployment's full URL (including `https://`).

**Auth:** a scoped API token minted in **Settings → API Tokens** (`scans:trigger` + `scans:read`), sent as `Authorization: Bearer blt_…`. Deployments fronted by Cloudflare Access ALSO require the `CF-Access-Client-Id` / `CF-Access-Client-Secret` service-token headers (optional fields on each request; leave blank for self-hosted).

**Try it:** set the **baseUrl** server variable to your deployment's URL, add a `blt_` token, then click **Send**. (A browser/proxy request cannot clear a Cloudflare Access edge without a service token.)

**Flow:** trigger (one target) → poll `GET /api/scans/{scanId}` until `COMPLETED` → download `GET /api/scans/{scanId}/export.sarif`.

baseUrl.description: Full base URL of your BreachLens deployment — include https:// and no trailing slash (e.g. https://breachlens.acme.com). Paste the whole URL; do not add a second https://.

tags.name: Scans
tags.description: Trigger scans, check status, export SARIF.

post.summary: Trigger a scan by GitHub repo (auto-onboard)
post.description: Auto-onboards the repository on first run — requires the BreachLens GitHub App installed on that account — then queues a scan. Requires the `scans:trigger` scope. Returns **503** if GitHub App auth is unavailable (App not installed / not authorized).

503.description: GitHub App auth unavailable — install/authorize the BreachLens GitHub App on the target account.

post.summary: Trigger a scan on an existing Repository
post.description: Queues a scan for a repository already known to BreachLens. Requires the `scans:trigger` scope plus DEVELOPER role or higher.

parameters.name: repoId
parameters.description: BreachLens Repository ID (from its detail-page URL).

post.summary: Trigger a scan on an existing Container
Trigger a scan on an existing Domain (DAST / pentest target)

get.description: Poll until `status` is a terminal state (`COMPLETED`, `FAILED`, or `CANCELLED`). The CLI polls every `--poll-interval` seconds. Active scans also include `currentPhase` and `currentPhasePct`. Requires the `scans:read` scope.

parameters.name: scanId
parameters.description: Scan job ID returned by a trigger call.

get.description: Returns a SARIF log for a COMPLETED scan — the standard format for GitHub Code Scanning, GitLab, Bitbucket, or Azure DevOps. Each result carries its BreachLens severity at `result.properties.severity` (CRITICAL | HIGH | MEDIUM | LOW | INFO) — the field the CLI counts for its `--severity-gate`. Requires the `scans:read` scope.

404.description: Scan not found or results not ready.

bearerAuth.description: Scoped API token from Settings → API Tokens (scopes: scans:trigger, scans:read).

CfAccessClientId.name: CF-Access-Client-Id
CfAccessClientId.description: Cloudflare Access service-token client id (ends in .access). Only for CF-Access-fronted deployments; leave blank for self-hosted.

CfAccessClientSecret.name: CF-Access-Client-Secret
CfAccessClientSecret.description: Cloudflare Access service-token secret. Pair it with CF-Access-Client-Id.

Unauthorized.description: Missing, invalid, or expired API token.

Forbidden.description: Token missing a required scope, or role-gate failure.

NotFound.description: Resource not found in your active org.

ScanTypes.description: Scan tiers to run. Omit for the server default (`SAST, SCA, SECRET, IAC` for repositories). Valid values depend on target type.

ResourceScanBody.description: Optional body. Omit to run the server default scan set for the target.

ScanTriggerResponse.description: The scan id is returned as `scanJobId` (aliased as `id` on some endpoints). `repository` is present only for from-github auto-discovery.

id.description: Alias for scanJobId on some endpoints.

newlyCreated.description: true when this call onboarded the repo for the first time.

status.description: Terminal states are COMPLETED, FAILED, and CANCELLED.

SarifLog.description: SARIF 2.1.0 static-analysis results log. Findings live under runs[].results[]; BreachLens severity is at result.properties.severity.
